extraction.one_line_summary: THOMAS Geraint won Tour de Suisse via 25.6km ITT in Vaduz, finishing 3 seconds behind stage winner EVENEPOEL but securing GC by 1:12 over IGITA.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL Remko destroyed second half of ITT course suggesting power advantage in headwind/hillier sections over pure flat speed.
KÜNG Stefan showing career-best climbing form while maintaining TT power signals equipment/training shift worth monitoring future stage races.
IGITA Sergio held second GC despite inferior TT pedigree by limiting losses at intermediate check rather than fading late.
MARTINEZ Dani using prototype disc brake TT bike indicates INEOS pre-Tour tech testing in race conditions.

rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: 54 km/h average with 104 second margin suggests Tour ITT threat if his stage race legs hold
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: 36-year-old winning at altitude/heat while thinking he could catch EVENEPOEL shows unexpected peak timing
rider_signals.KÜNG Stefan: positive: climbing into top-5 GC while maintaining elite TT speed represents complete rider evolution from pure specialist
rider_signals.IGITA Sergio: neutral: held second GC despite mechanical concerns for teammate POWLESS showing mental resilience under pressure
rider_signals.VAN WILDER Ilan: negative: unable to finish ITT suggests serious physical issue for young QUICKSTEP rider to monitor
rider_signals.JUNGELS Bob: positive: fifth on stage described as renaissance signals return to form worth tracking for Grand Tour support
